purpose: Per-substrate variance estimate via 3 independent LoRA seed runs. Single-seed Φ measurements conflate substrate effect with seed noise; 3-seed ensemble enables Var(Φ_substrate) decomposition.

seed_rationale.123: lo-entropy near-sequential — checks LoRA init non-degeneracy

ensemble_design.seed_independence_note: All 3 seeds share base model + corpus + hyperparams; ONLY torch.manual_seed + np.random.seed + LoRA A/B init seed differ.
ensemble_design.applies_to_substrates: all paths in config/phi_4path_substrates.json (4) and config/phi_8path_substrates_spec.json (8 if K56 active)

interpretation.low_cv: CV < 0.10 → substrate Φ stable, cross-substrate Δ trustworthy
interpretation.med_cv: 0.10 ≤ CV < 0.25 → seed noise non-trivial, report Phi_mean ± Std
interpretation.high_cv: CV ≥ 0.25 → seed effect dominates, increase n_seeds to 5+ before claiming substrate-invariance

cross_substrate_comparison_with_variance.preferred: Bayes Factor (BF10) over Welch's t for n=3 small-sample setting
cross_substrate_comparison_with_variance.formula_bf_note: BF10 via Jeffrey-Zellner-Siow prior; |BF10| > 3 = moderate evidence of difference

gate_integration.extended_gate_with_variance: |Phi_mean(p_a) - Phi_mean(p_b)| / Phi_mean_overall <= 0.05 AND CV(p_a) AND CV(p_b) < 0.25
gate_integration.rationale: single-seed gate can pass spuriously when seed noise > inter-substrate diff. CV constraint prevents this.
gate_integration.fail_action: increase n_seeds to 5; if CV still ≥ 0.25 → declare substrate-NOT-invariant for that pair

compute_cost_estimate.h100_hours_per_run_estimate: ~6h (8B base, anima r13 corpus, 3 epoch, rank 64)
compute_cost_estimate.amortization_note: K59 autoscale can compress wall-time at +cost; K60 distributed_eval shortens eval loop separately.

audit_notes.fake_pass_guard: Spec-only — no LoRA training has been launched. Variance numbers will be empirical post-activation.
audit_notes.uchg_note: Not uchg-locked; seed list may be revised pre-activation.
